description: Women's variant of the wide-fit Reflex, combining low volume with generous toe room and medium heel width for female climbers seeking accommodation over specialization. An ideal beginner shoe for women with average to wider foot shapes prioritizing comfort on extended gym sessions.

sizing: Leather stretches 0.5 EU; size down from street size. Lace allows fine-tuning. Moderate break-in expected (2-3 sessions).
description: The laced interpretation of the Vapor platform, offering precise fit control via lacing while retaining the versatile edge-focused design ideal for trad multi-pitch and sport climbing. Leather upper and XS Edge rubber ensure durability, while the center-shaped last accommodates diverse foot structures.

sizing: Low-volume fit ideal for narrow to medium-narrow feet. Minimal stretch. Size snug from day one.
description: A low-volume variant of the Vapor platform, designed for climbers with narrower feet seeking versatile edge-focused performance without bulk. Maintains the durable XS Grip 2 rubber and center-shaped last while reducing volume and weight for precise, responsive climbing.

The softest, stickiest shoe in the Vapor family. Where the V uses hard XS Edge for edging, the S runs full-length XS Grip 2 for grip and sensitivity — yet the Bi-Tension rand and full midsole keep it surprisingly stable underfoot. Reviewers consistently note it edges better than the rubber suggests, while smearing and toe hooking are in a different league. The removable Nano Strap doubles as a slipper when you want zero bulk.

Women's take on the Vapor S, pairing the same sticky XS Grip 2 sole and M50 toe patch with a lower-volume last. The Bi-Tension system gives it more structure than the rubber softness implies — strong enough to edge on thin holds, sensitive enough to trust on smeary volumes. A genuine do-everything shoe for intermediate to advanced female climbers.

sizing: Runs 0.5 EU size large due to leather stretch. Size down 0.5 EU from street shoe. Moderate break-in expected.
description: A leather-upper workhorse for sport climbing, the Vapor V pairs real hide with Vibram's versatile XS Edge rubber and a full midsole designed to shine on slabs and edges. The center-shaped last suits climbers with Morton's toe or longer second toes, making it the thinking person's all-rounder.

sizing: Women's-specific fit with low volume and narrow heel. Leather stretches 0.5 EU; size down accordingly. Expect 2-3 sessions break-in.
description: Women's version of the Vapor V, tailored with lower volume and narrower heel while maintaining the same leather durability and center-shaped last philosophy. Ideal for female climbers seeking a leather shoe that combines breathability with precise edge control across varied terrain.

The women's lace Vapor — Scarpa's answer for female trad and sport climbers who want the Vapor platform with granular fit control. XS Edge rubber and a full midsole prioritize edging and support over sensitivity, making it the workhorse choice for long routes where you need to trust tiny footholds pitch after pitch.

sizing: Standard volume, medium width; minimal stretch. Size to a snug fit; expect minimal break-in.
description: An affordable entry to performance climbing, the Veloce pairs slight downturn with sticky S72 rubber and a partial midsole built for intermediate gym climbers and casual outdoor days. Wide-fit last and accessible price make it an excellent beginner-to-intermediate shoe for building technique on indoor walls.

The lace version of the Veloce takes Scarpa's gym-focused platform and adds real fit tunability. The offset lace pattern tracks the shoe's slight asymmetry, while the M50 rand wraps the forefoot for toe hooks you wouldn't expect from a comfort shoe. S72 rubber is the star — stickier than most performance compounds on plastic, though it pays for that grip with faster outdoor wear.

Women's lace Veloce, offering the same sticky S72 sole in a lower-volume build. The lace closure is the main draw over the velcro version — it adapts to narrower or wider forefeet without the all-or-nothing fit of a strap. At 350g it's one of the lighter lace shoes in the range, built for gym climbers who want adjustability without weight.

The lightest shoe in the Veloce family, designed for women who climb indoors regularly and want something they can throw on between routes without fuss. The S72 sole grips plastic better than shoes twice its price, while the single velcro keeps weight down. Not a crag shoe — but for gym sessions and intro outdoor days, it punches above its class.
